description:
SPEC-clone-layout has accumulated structural problems that three small edits will not resolve. After design discussion, this ball expands to a SPEC rewrite of §3, §4, §5, §6, §7, §10, §11.

## What changed in the design

The current SPEC keys identity in two layers via sha256-truncated hashes: <origin-key> from the canonical origin URL, <path-hash> from the absolute git-dir path. Hashing violates the transparency ethos — `~/.local/state/balls/e9f3a7c4.../4a2c8f1d.../worktrees/bl-abcd/` is inscrutable. It also forces the SPEC to invent a "synthetic origin-key" fallback for stealth/no-git mode, which is the smell that the layering is over-engineered for a problem that doesn't exist.

The replacement: no hashing, no two-layer keying, nested paths from the natural identifiers. One file has one parent.

```
~/.local/state/balls/trackers/<pct-enc-origin-url>/<pct-enc-branch>/   # state-repo checkout
  .balls/{tasks,project.json,plugins}/
  tracker.json     # redirect pointer; absent or empty here means "this IS the tracker"

~/.local/state/balls/worktrees/<nested-repo-path>/<bl-id>/             # task worktrees
                                                                        # e.g. home/mark/dev/balls/bl-abcd/

~/.config/balls/<nested-repo-path>/clone.json                          # local override; only when present

# repo.json: committed, on the repo's own balls/tasks (per-code-repo policy)
# project.json: committed, on the tracker's balls/tasks (tracker-scope: id_length, version,
#               min_bl_version, plugins, plus optional defaults for layered fields)
# In the solo case repo.json and project.json coexist on the same branch at distinct paths.
```

## Key decisions

1. **No hashes anywhere.** Identity comes from the natural names: the canonical origin URL, the branch name, the clone's filesystem path. Hand-readable, find-able, transparent.

2. **Percent-encoding for URL and branch.** Each becomes one path component (so `balls/tasks` doesn't fan out into a nondeterministic subtree, and so `..` in a foreign string can't escape). URL-safe, reversible, stdlib-supported.

3. **Nested clone path.** `/home/mark/dev/balls` → `home/mark/dev/balls/` under the worktrees/clone-config prefixes. Single-parent property holds, hand-operable with `cd`.

4. **Three config files, three scopes, one shadowing rule.**
   - `project.json` (tracker-scope, committed on tracker branch): id_length, version, min_bl_version, plugins. PLUS optional project-wide defaults for layered fields. These describe the *shared task store's shape*; federating repos cannot disagree.
   - `repo.json` (per-code-repo, committed on the repo's own balls/tasks): integrate.mode, review.gate_command, require_remote_on_{claim,review,close}, protected_main, auto_fetch_on_ready, stale_threshold_seconds, worktree_dir.
   - `clone.json` (per-on-disk-checkout, never committed): override layer. Same schema as repo.json for layered fields. Tracker-scope fields rejected outside project.json.
   - Precedence for layered fields: `clone.json ?? repo.json ?? project.json ?? built-in default`.

5. **Stealth lives in clone.json.** `bl init --stealth` writes a clone.json with `stealth: true` and `tasks_dir: <path>`. bl reads clone.json first; if stealth, skip the origin/tracker.json/redirect machinery entirely. No special-case in the layout — clone.json's path identity is the canonical absolute --tasks-dir (or PWD) under the same nested `~/.config/balls/<nested-path>/` scheme.

6. **Redirect mechanism unchanged in shape, simpler in implementation.** Every bl op stats `~/.local/state/balls/trackers/<enc-origin>/<enc-balls-tasks>/tracker.json`. Absent (or absent `state_url`) → this location IS the tracker. Present → fetch that tracker's URL/branch into `trackers/<...>/` and use it as active. Single hop; chained redirect aborts.

## Scope of the SPEC rewrite

- §3 Layout: replace the hashed two-layer tree with the nested transparent layout above.
- §4 Identity: replace the <origin-key> / <path-hash> derivation with "clone path is the identity; origin URL keys the tracker; both percent-encoded." Stealth gets one paragraph (clone.json carries the flag), not its own section.
- §5 Bootstrap convention: tracker.json's location moves from "on the repo's own balls/tasks" to "at the trackers/<enc-origin>/<enc-branch>/ path." The single-hop rule and "branch name is constant" property survive.
- §6 Config files: project.json scope-rule (tracker-scope, not shadowable for those fields) is articulated as "describes the shared task store's shape." Three-layer shadowing rule for layered fields. clone.json schema gains `stealth` and `tasks_dir`.
- §7 Materialization: simplified — no two-layer key derivation, just `mkdir -p` of the nested trees.
- §10 Hand-operability: drastically shorter. No sha256sum, no hash truncation. Just URL canonicalization + percent-encoding + nested mkdir.
- §11 Migration: covers the bl-fc50 → this-shape delta (which is now larger than bl-fc50 expected).
- §14 Conformance tests: revise the hashing-related tests (§14.2 origin-key derivation, §14.3 per-clone isolation) to test the path-based identity instead.

## Out of scope

- No code changes in this ball. The bl-77cb implementation work that landed in commit f60262d (vendored SHA-256 + clone-identity hashing) is now scoped to be reverted as part of the implementation ball that follows this SPEC revision; not this one.
- No phase rescoping. The four implementation phases (bl-77cb, bl-717e, bl-05e5, bl-a84c) remain as filed; bl-77cb's slice gets re-planned against the rewritten SPEC.
- No rename of bl-77cb. The reverted commit lands as part of the next bl-77cb slice, not as a separate ball.

## Why the original three-edit scope was wrong

The three open questions in the original description — §4 vs §10 lowercase scope, unnamed <path-hash> breadcrumb, stealth/no-git under XDG — each pointed at the same underlying smell: the hash-based two-layer identity is doing work the SPEC doesn't need it to do. Resolving the three locally would have papered over the structural issue. The design discussion (in the chat that filed this update) converged on dropping the hashing entirely, which collapses all three questions to "doesn't apply anymore" while simplifying the SPEC substantially.
title: SPEC-clone-layout: resolve open questions surfaced while implementing Phase 1
